race.name: Erzgebirge 24-Hour Ski Race
race.tagline: Twenty-four hours on a 6km loop in the Ore Mountains — a niche ultra-endurance format where skiers chase maximum distance through the night.

climate.description: The Erzgebirge (Ore Mountains) on the German-Czech border with 300+ km of groomed trails. Moderate winter conditions for Central Europe.
challenges: Moderate snow reliability at this altitude

course.primary: 6km loop course in the Erzgebirge (Ore Mountains). Skiers complete as many laps as possible in 24 hours.
features: Erzgebirge has 300+ km of groomed trails

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Unique ultra-endurance format that stands alone in the Nordic racing world, but extremely niche with minimal field size.

history.summary: The Erzgebirge 24-Hour Ski Race is a niche ultra-endurance event in Saxony's Ore Mountains on the German-Czech border. Participants ski as many laps of a 6km loop as possible in 24 hours.
notable_facts: Erzgebirge has 300+ km of groomed trails
Night skiing through German-Czech border mountains
